claim: Vibe coding is a valid software engineering methodology for production systems

factual_accuracy.reason: Document B provides precise definitions and verifiable citations (e.g., Karpathy Feb 2025 post, Veracode 45% vulnerability stat, Thoughtworks/Capgemini/New Stack articles all confirmed accurate via searches), with only minor discrepancy on JetBrains stat (actual 2025 adoption ~85%, not 72% non-use); Document A makes general claims without citations but aligns with consensus.

balance.reason: Document B extensively acknowledges counterarguments with dedicated sections on validity contexts (table), augmentation conditions, industry adoption stats, and Cost of Failure framing; Document A is more dismissive with a brief table.

structure.reason: Document B has superior organization with numbered sections, subsections, tables, clear progression from definition to evidence to roadmap; Document A is concise but less detailed and tabular.

practical_value.reason: Document B offers highly actionable 5-point augmentation controls, CoF principle, and specific empirical experiments (e.g., MTTR, defect density studies); Document A provides a single improved claim but lacks depth.

overall_winner.reason: Document B excels across all criteria with rigorous, cited analysis, balance, structure, and practical roadmap superior to the generic, uncited Document A.
